subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the concept of health including physical, mental, and social well-being
Evaluate how lifestyle, environment, and emotions affect health
Differentiate between communicable and non-communicable diseases with examples
Analyze disease transmission pathways and preventive strategies
Interpret data related to disease spread and public health issues
Explain immunity and the scientific role of vaccines
Evaluate the impact of antibiotic misuse and antibiotic resistance

lesson_plan.title: Health and Well-being: A Multidimensional Concept
topics_covered: Impact of emotions and relationships on health
student_activities: Analyze the story of a student experiencing loneliness and health issues
Create a concept map linking physical, mental, and social health

lesson_plan.title: Healthy Lifestyle and Behavioral Factors
student_activities: Identify unhealthy habits and propose solutions

lesson_plan.title: Environmental Health and Sanitation
student_activities: Observe surroundings and identify environmental health risks

lesson_plan.title: Disease Diagnosis: Symptoms and Signs
student_activities: Analyze health scenarios and identify symptoms and signs

lesson_plan.title: Communicable Diseases and Transmission Analysis
topics_covered: Examples such as malaria, dengue, and cholera
student_activities: Create a transmission model for a communicable disease

lesson_plan.title: Non-Communicable Diseases and Data Analysis
topics_covered: Risk factors such as diet, obesity, and inactivity
student_activities: Analyze dengue case data and create a bar graph
Discuss environmental causes for seasonal disease patterns

lesson_plan.title: Immunity, Vaccination, and Antibiotic Resistance
student_activities: Prepare a poster on vaccination awareness

teacher_tip: Encourage students to apply scientific thinking by analyzing real-world health issues such as vaccination programs, disease outbreaks, and antibiotic resistance.
